Today, I’m writing to let you know about a special offer for you from Hillsdale College, a private, fiercely independent, conservative college located in south-central Michigan where I work. You may have heard of us on talk radio, social media, YouTube, podcasts, or even on _Fox News_ because of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty—like our free online courses. __ And though we’re best known for educating Americans about the Constitution and founding principles through these free courses, one of our most popular courses has been about a famous _British_ author—C.S. Lewis. In fact, our free online course, **“[ An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance,](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=a424545d9bc947718344946600d59f9b&_e=nZwcI-bjNl1wfCUNIKn3CEJQfNHi4FRFkWoHYQI_o8iJKMDyZxh2AcOfcSlkGi33DI7dJtLQKWAfRi6jZZpOFcv0gNddDBwISN7YNtHp0Hd7RUEwjy0dHNzoBIZ4J4dnVx3g0RbesEiGxbAXiNYctfuElIDDlw0hOXZXVnmaU9Kgh4v2tMPSptica-n-pyxOW0_alLU2shjLyFRkaJr_XXviwvJfsJ5-JZUUgCc2_2Ro3mtR3J9Eawrd0EuqFABbzTG56V712_CQY_6k4Vq9w95adRg5dDRpSYRdgDJWEWv-4-_flRO1qTQM-HK5rIA-VTVIHK9jEBaZ7UqGodeuw5e2hJYl2_KoUBA545M6P_2lANKuQT9tCNrmmwQwAz5t53COpJJ9k2jHZn2RFNY8Pjke1lAAMwdhAWsN32LUFclzc0z-ntdkFmxy0JfgxyOK)” **has more than 500,000 people enrolled. C.S. Lewis was a war hero, a bright light during some very dark hours in world history. He is one of my absolute favorite authors because... ...While you may know him for his landmark _Chronicles of Narnia_ series, he was also a brilliant philosopher, writer, and Christian apologist. That’s why we decided to develop this online course. Since launching **“[An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=a424545d9bc947718344946600d59f9b&_e=nZwcI-bjNl1wfCUNIKn3CEJQfNHi4FRFkWoHYQI_o8iJKMDyZxh2AcOfcSlkGi33DI7dJtLQKWAfRi6jZZpOFcv0gNddDBwISN7YNtHp0Hd7RUEwjy0dHNzoBIZ4J4dnVx3g0RbesEiGxbAXiNYctfuElIDDlw0hOXZXVnmaU9Kgh4v2tMPSptica-n-pyxOW0_alLU2shjLyFRkaJr_XXviwvJfsJ5-JZUUgCc2_2Ro3mtR3J9Eawrd0EuqFABbzTG56V712_CQY_6k4Vq9w95adRg5dDRpSYRdgDJWEWs5x4xI7c1_zkcjK64vK9zAek3ozJskaUepBSs7USBRpfGmQgfYhLE7E9Rh41GqZ4A3a4YEFl6iQnszc6B60OA0-N0ClJ2KAVMAQ8wiWbcMbwIhWx52IQZpRAEZ3qP9SyBoUFLZuarAVLmbO9RcSWCU),” **we’ve received an outpouring of thanks for offering a course that covers the breadth and scope of Lewis’ writings. [](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=a424545d9bc947718344946600d59f9b&_e=nZwcI-bjNl1wfCUNIKn3CEJQfNHi4FRFkWoHYQI_o8iJKMDyZxh2AcOfcSlkGi33DI7dJtLQKWAfRi6jZZpOFcv0gNddDBwISN7YNtHp0Hd7RUEwjy0dHNzoBIZ4J4dnVx3g0RbesEiGxbAXiNYctfuElIDDlw0hOXZXVnmaU9Kgh4v2tMPSptica-n-pyxOW0_alLU2shjLyFRkaJr_XXviwvJfsJ5-JZUUgCc2_2Ro3mtR3J9Eawrd0EuqFABbzTG56V712_CQY_6k4Vq9w95adRg5dDRpSYRdgDJWEWsd4-ZYD4qmKFUNNWFmVvJXsZ215CaPAj9EmJ8p9vgEW4Ivs9DEb0Hr91kahs3vGzIBe0-0kJJggBNuAitHiJGqEWxaDOtT9f9FqTLzSp72NjgJh1g0wW4qFi07Z9O6yfjdT1O6TlESqrsmNTkjWBmM) We’ve also heard from many of our friends that they would love to own a DVD version. So, I’m pleased to announce that the C.S. Lewis course is available on DVD for your home library. You can also give it as a gift or use it in a small group, such as a Bible study or book club. name: Hillsdale College via Defend the Constitution
subject: Reserve your DVD box set
body:
One of our most popular courses has been about a famous British author — C.S. Lewis.

This sponsored message from Defend the Constitution is on behalf of Hillsdale College
[](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=296a339a1eb346b7b1bc990e3b9d3521&_e=qaScOGADRAgBwwjX76rTINjRebhqnctFy6mIcdqsb7c4HJDVFFUoBvvIDPj1ETaI26YkHC7w1BV7vYGORgWDf94xp9czp2T1mG2QiqyNYCz8blTzMYA3tqx5r00fGfJG0VvJn9DOXcyVmq2GVxVdWbJwlRzd6A9zdzrSMW45ZsAu_xCXwTPZAalMTbyYCGdFK7zo8aMLtnWIixZPsRatnURiwHnYUyEA81AhlU6dHDHARMumF1PfbgPoUC86wy5KIOnjD4EytX85cKDD0kTBtCew7rvgnqOsaHNExxhEPDYY-kVJ3L1AeozeLU5wS3VoHTkoWakAn75M1z2a-Ryg4aIzoAlvjiViflD7S4n5JfuBPImo0wb6i5vVoPRcmrqr-iu_UF93Ny7WfAcVK1dGxUpITejwOLSdJXDYPs91CUE8DvQp4881CClplKPW-iEZ) Dear Fellow American, I hope you are having a fine day so far. Thanks for opening my email. Today, I’m writing to let you know about a special offer for you from Hillsdale College, a private, fiercely independent, conservative college located in south-central Michigan where I work. You may have heard of us on talk radio, social media, YouTube, podcasts, or even on _Fox News_ because of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty—like our free online courses. __ And though we’re best known for educating Americans about the Constitution and founding principles through these free courses, one of our most popular courses has been about a famous _British_ author—C.S. Lewis. In fact, our free online course, **“[ An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance,](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=296a339a1eb346b7b1bc990e3b9d3521&_e=qaScOGADRAgBwwjX76rTINjRebhqnctFy6mIcdqsb7c4HJDVFFUoBvvIDPj1ETaI26YkHC7w1BV7vYGORgWDf94xp9czp2T1mG2QiqyNYCz8blTzMYA3tqx5r00fGfJG0VvJn9DOXcyVmq2GVxVdWbJwlRzd6A9zdzrSMW45ZsAu_xCXwTPZAalMTbyYCGdFK7zo8aMLtnWIixZPsRatnURiwHnYUyEA81AhlU6dHDHARMumF1PfbgPoUC86wy5KIOnjD4EytX85cKDD0kTBtCew7rvgnqOsaHNExxhEPDZ06KIzFOLuZWAIB7f2jxzIWnZFj3p-IOKzgzOWZ75NL5doe8HhY_s_SDV2tMhQ3CNwpJ94dnjEc73oy-t09w_W5NsHX0erIPfSe02UYCIl3QEJH01X9-HqDxkCQWV3DdOZgqTLYopjNon9ndqEUFYJ)” **has more than 500,000 people enrolled. C.S. Lewis was a war hero, a bright light during some very dark hours in world history. He is one of my absolute favorite authors because... ...While you may know him for his landmark _Chronicles of Narnia_ series, he was also a brilliant philosopher, writer, and Christian apologist. That’s why we decided to develop this online course. Since launching **“[An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=296a339a1eb346b7b1bc990e3b9d3521&_e=qaScOGADRAgBwwjX76rTINjRebhqnctFy6mIcdqsb7c4HJDVFFUoBvvIDPj1ETaI26YkHC7w1BV7vYGORgWDf94xp9czp2T1mG2QiqyNYCz8blTzMYA3tqx5r00fGfJG0VvJn9DOXcyVmq2GVxVdWbJwlRzd6A9zdzrSMW45ZsAu_xCXwTPZAalMTbyYCGdFK7zo8aMLtnWIixZPsRatnURiwHnYUyEA81AhlU6dHDHARMumF1PfbgPoUC86wy5KIOnjD4EytX85cKDD0kTBtCew7rvgnqOsaHNExxhEPDai-W5t1I-L_DRzBihva2bbhRkATuSSkN6gKoB4o2isPPSD3ATi8yi2BIBNseDQa3NiPX-xRiBLZnJu2b222Mb4AkW1h1PBnfr8ZpYjz1rY79edmezk-V98wqvK_W6GfKDDHXYa0ZvD_bglCCQ5_SXX),” **we’ve received an outpouring of thanks for offering a course that covers the breadth and scope of Lewis’ writings. [](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=296a339a1eb346b7b1bc990e3b9d3521&_e=qaScOGADRAgBwwjX76rTINjRebhqnctFy6mIcdqsb7c4HJDVFFUoBvvIDPj1ETaI26YkHC7w1BV7vYGORgWDf94xp9czp2T1mG2QiqyNYCz8blTzMYA3tqx5r00fGfJG0VvJn9DOXcyVmq2GVxVdWbJwlRzd6A9zdzrSMW45ZsAu_xCXwTPZAalMTbyYCGdFK7zo8aMLtnWIixZPsRatnURiwHnYUyEA81AhlU6dHDHARMumF1PfbgPoUC86wy5KIOnjD4EytX85cKDD0kTBtCew7rvgnqOsaHNExxhEPDbQgLyX7D5V0S-4rbQ6_Z4PXbf5Hj-4jKSeVuzozWmGZlvhefka-0DSTH0-K6rXCXlZnQ51tlNZMLBL6DX7kGFbBYg2AaC6j76TCkqmDgFnEYiCzdinTOfjqsekQPmsbgPPTuH7alk0qgpH3MR-hUo4) We’ve also heard from many of our friends that they would love to own a DVD version. So, I’m pleased to announce that the C.S. Lewis course is available on DVD for your home library. You can also give it as a gift or use it in a small group, such as a Bible study or book club. We only made a few of these special sets, so get this premium edition of **“[An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=296a339a1eb346b7b1bc990e3b9d3521&_e=qaScOGADRAgBwwjX76rTINjRebhqnctFy6mIcdqsb7c4HJDVFFUoBvvIDPj1ETaI26YkHC7w1BV7vYGORgWDf94xp9czp2T1mG2QiqyNYCz8blTzMYA3tqx5r00fGfJG0VvJn9DOXcyVmq2GVxVdWbJwlRzd6A9zdzrSMW45ZsAu_xCXwTPZAalMTbyYCGdFK7zo8aMLtnWIixZPsRatnURiwHnYUyEA81AhlU6dHDHARMumF1PfbgPoUC86wy5KIOnjD4EytX85cKDD0kTBtCew7rvgnqOsaHNExxhEPDarJ0DPtyTfab9GJDJeciuls566ogJVcSP1_EEhmZoZAH_jZWSYCvGToaHHfma4O61oUCK_JGdan-QxHnpGISQ2zQSGBcsFF89AIYGcJWhHhcO3h5q7HgG6KRUzOiLQtbr2G3b9-vSTvcL3CxPugNAR)”** on DVD today. clean_body: One of our most popular courses has been about a famous British author — C.S. Lewis. This sponsored message from Defend the Constitution is on behalf of Hillsdale College Dear Fellow American, I hope you are having a fine day so far. Thanks for opening my email. Today, I’m writing to let you know about a special offer for you from Hillsdale College, a private, fiercely independent, conservative college located in south-central Michigan where I work. You may have heard of us on talk radio, social media, YouTube, podcasts, or even on Fox News because of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty—like our free online courses. And though we’re best known for educating Americans about the Constitution and founding principles through these free courses, one of our most popular courses has been about a famous British author—C.S. Lewis. In fact, our free online course, “” has more than 500,000 people enrolled. C.S. Lewis was a war hero, a bright light during some very dark hours in world history. He is one of my absolute favorite authors because... ...While you may know him for his landmark Chronicles of Narnia series, he was also a brilliant philosopher, writer, and Christian apologist. That’s why we decided to develop this online course. Since launching “,” we’ve received an outpouring of thanks for offering a course that covers the breadth and scope of Lewis’ writings. We’ve also heard from many of our friends that they would love to own a DVD version. So, I’m pleased to announce that the C.S. Lewis course is available on DVD for your home library. You can also give it as a gift or use it in a small group, such as a Bible study or book club. We only made a few of these special sets, so get this premium edition of “” on DVD today. Given how popular this course is, the DVD box set won’t last long. Reserve your DVD set now using this secure link, while they last: Warm regards, Bill Gray, ‘01 Vice President Institutional Advancement Hillsdale College Defend the Constitution PO Box 1188 Fort Collins, CO 80522 | _

| Peter, I joined Morning Joe to discuss Trump’s selection of Tulsi Gabbard for Director of National Intelligence: [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td9002cd5-9488-400f-a680-963093bb36f9/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) As a former CIA case officer and Member of Congress, I’ve taken an oath to defend the U.S. Constitution. I also know that the DNI role is not something to take lightly – especially as the U.S. faces threats from adversaries around the world. Tulsi Gabbard is not only unprepared and unqualified for this role, but she has spread conspiracy theories, doubted the credibility of the U.S. intelligence community, and cozied up with dictators like Vladimir Putin and Bashar al-Assad. I’m not speaking out because of my political party — I’m speaking out on behalf of America’s national security. Throughout my life in service, I have dedicated myself to safeguarding our country, its principles, and its Constitution. As Governor of Virginia, I pledge to continue protecting our democracy, preserving our fundamental rights, and never wavering in the defense of our freedoms. [I hope that you’ll support my efforts with a split contribution between my campaign and Blue Amp Action as I continue this work as Governor of Virginia:](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tce29ceaa-07b8-4352-a848-82186c23bcad/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

---
| | | Thank you,
Abigail Spanberger

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4e7bc30d-b0a5-46a0-a356-169c035a6dbd/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

body:
One of our most popular courses has been about a famous British author — C.S. Lewis.

This sponsored message from Full Mag News is on behalf of Hillsdale College
[](https://links.fullmagnews.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4e5978b878ff468fae8fb47d538be3d2&_e=Yfv_NQzVu8A87bJTa7bpPmYz5cnasX7dEI8JJdhqmgCDqxWoMUk2VEfkUjr8dS3A74TGNApqlK2vttP6KwQjvwZL28ygn24g85t6jyIZ_hvHvxTZU9L2Z8HMJBqNt6hz7ifVM_k_7dQkuOHMsaVHjPy6shNKwGVVpkmYy7jnVKQ4qYE3CF2cvdYqVSSJZQJhPfE3nsL6ERwHQ9PaU3KVyyvHTON7tS-6aP90tCGxbMJAJXCyfoHTkWVbYc2ICrNat1dvsgx41bUv8TPTMl4u3JJ9nG-ZjdowUho8jp4k5DP-2pSwYNKuBjLHkrA2pPsXGy8Q9Hp-4f8sw2M_y9P20JJ9l7sslRlr6FTo_t2RBE5G9iBL4o-LlymvG5IUAqUuToZhV3mKX7kXyiIGLzBJoKrghzJN3dEsdDZ_AMkwPah0veLvKhFYMVf5plVaXOIj) Dear Fellow American, I hope you are having a fine day so far. Thanks for opening my email. Today, I’m writing to let you know about a special offer for you from Hillsdale College, a private, fiercely independent, conservative college located in south-central Michigan where I work. You may have heard of us on talk radio, social media, YouTube, podcasts, or even on _Fox News_ because of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty—like our free online courses. __ And though we’re best known for educating Americans about the Constitution and founding principles through these free courses, one of our most popular courses has been about a famous _British_ author—C.S. Lewis. In fact, our free online course, **“[ An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance,](https://links.fullmagnews.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4e5978b878ff468fae8fb47d538be3d2&_e=Yfv_NQzVu8A87bJTa7bpPmYz5cnasX7dEI8JJdhqmgCDqxWoMUk2VEfkUjr8dS3A74TGNApqlK2vttP6KwQjvwZL28ygn24g85t6jyIZ_hvHvxTZU9L2Z8HMJBqNt6hz7ifVM_k_7dQkuOHMsaVHjPy6shNKwGVVpkmYy7jnVKQ4qYE3CF2cvdYqVSSJZQJhPfE3nsL6ERwHQ9PaU3KVyyvHTON7tS-6aP90tCGxbMJAJXCyfoHTkWVbYc2ICrNat1dvsgx41bUv8TPTMl4u3JJ9nG-ZjdowUho8jp4k5DMfkuxAgWaoCtKd3OfeYxj44I-1M1_wLffhbxqGJhjOhk9krFn084E2-PW0U_LjqXI5StII099vJVmF4Lsh6SpBfRuRkZ4LQp3OCAkSTLwplTuEkBe1GnwSafsE1isQCZInAsKtlISdZ1KByArN-ft_)” **has more than 500,000 people enrolled. C.S. Lewis was a war hero, a bright light during some very dark hours in world history. He is one of my absolute favorite authors because... ...While you may know him for his landmark _Chronicles of Narnia_ series, he was also a brilliant philosopher, writer, and Christian apologist. That’s why we decided to develop this online course. Since launching **“[An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance](https://links.fullmagnews.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4e5978b878ff468fae8fb47d538be3d2&_e=Yfv_NQzVu8A87bJTa7bpPmYz5cnasX7dEI8JJdhqmgCDqxWoMUk2VEfkUjr8dS3A74TGNApqlK2vttP6KwQjvwZL28ygn24g85t6jyIZ_hvHvxTZU9L2Z8HMJBqNt6hz7ifVM_k_7dQkuOHMsaVHjPy6shNKwGVVpkmYy7jnVKQ4qYE3CF2cvdYqVSSJZQJhPfE3nsL6ERwHQ9PaU3KVyyvHTON7tS-6aP90tCGxbMJAJXCyfoHTkWVbYc2ICrNat1dvsgx41bUv8TPTMl4u3JJ9nG-ZjdowUho8jp4k5DP__V1j6bcDObVj-IfMuqjn6t85KcXbNrdFNM_q1s_MKPZs1Mlf-EZUXIx3dMfOGvcXK4XQIGQqDXZv1gLW5xlSbr_Ibk_mHzIXh_9ZcDQxe9Rq_LITh0jvfBMXsXIp7jY2SGrhW6LqJQLmP3HTLb6O),” **we’ve received an outpouring of thanks for offering a course that covers the breadth and scope of Lewis’ writings. [](https://links.fullmagnews.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4e5978b878ff468fae8fb47d538be3d2&_e=Yfv_NQzVu8A87bJTa7bpPmYz5cnasX7dEI8JJdhqmgCDqxWoMUk2VEfkUjr8dS3A74TGNApqlK2vttP6KwQjvwZL28ygn24g85t6jyIZ_hvHvxTZU9L2Z8HMJBqNt6hz7ifVM_k_7dQkuOHMsaVHjPy6shNKwGVVpkmYy7jnVKQ4qYE3CF2cvdYqVSSJZQJhPfE3nsL6ERwHQ9PaU3KVyyvHTON7tS-6aP90tCGxbMJAJXCyfoHTkWVbYc2ICrNat1dvsgx41bUv8TPTMl4u3JJ9nG-ZjdowUho8jp4k5DN9qucgWZDGN3uS_vw7OoWz9A0zOLN6qGcWyWcg12mVmbSCUuZriiQdgpIdLlouygoU8cbUbmBb9pwPgIxlGy4knJ9EfY2-vkpjTc_Dx8mcJr6-9BDhPvt4LiWYKTlNE1kPS6mdCxmTqxwA__D_QYlF) We’ve also heard from many of our friends that they would love to own a DVD version. So, I’m pleased to announce that the C.S. Lewis course is available on DVD for your home library. You can also give it as a gift or use it in a small group, such as a Bible study or book club. We only made a few of these special sets, so get this premium edition of **“[An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance](https://links.fullmagnews.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4e5978b878ff468fae8fb47d538be3d2&_e=Yfv_NQzVu8A87bJTa7bpPmYz5cnasX7dEI8JJdhqmgCDqxWoMUk2VEfkUjr8dS3A74TGNApqlK2vttP6KwQjvwZL28ygn24g85t6jyIZ_hvHvxTZU9L2Z8HMJBqNt6hz7ifVM_k_7dQkuOHMsaVHjPy6shNKwGVVpkmYy7jnVKQ4qYE3CF2cvdYqVSSJZQJhPfE3nsL6ERwHQ9PaU3KVyyvHTON7tS-6aP90tCGxbMJAJXCyfoHTkWVbYc2ICrNat1dvsgx41bUv8TPTMl4u3JJ9nG-ZjdowUho8jp4k5DOmBrwBFFKaLLqIryZTYOzCrDWliDs2EILSHZF8YxZL-oNupk3ZPSdlWB5XF1T7uY4ZuzjeA_fcDjzRVvlcAME1xry2Em3FdfZ7QFgwasFIshalar_0cjP2Av001fycEvdzohqUVj2bLJOTMfi7oX1Z)”** on DVD today. * On MONDAY midday, attend the INAUGURATION of the 47th President in Washington, DC!

# Wednesday, January 8, 2025
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1919, ballerina [Nana Gollner](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0z7FuwyAQANCvgS3WcRiCB4Yu3tK1o3U2ZxuVQIRRnPx91e79gSe9r9t0o5hi3j7pzr691jIhoLmAuoCbAsX0nnoZ_AxhUJK9umqrtXEO5O5pVle76MVwzwsO1s5LCDoMztGMzEZG_4uBAqeUBgMdkDKrHiCgVUyrFT1wiK3Uru3c-EXHmt7lybVbyl0mv7f2OIT-EDgKHM_z7NqxU8kpZu5K3QSOO-Uwl_ItcOTcauRD4LiVlDLXS6ZMsvrwOGNK8bBX0cN2p5j-_PZv-OnxJwAA__8tPVmN) was born in El Paso. She made her first professional appearance in Los Angeles at the age of 14, and she later became the first American to gain prima ballerina roles in a European company, performing in classical ballets such as _Giselle_ and _Swan Lake_. The winter weather is coming, and Texans are [taking to social media](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOszAQBOCnMR1obYOBwsXf0OVvr0QbWAdLxkb2Kk7e_pT0V89oPml-busNffDx8R9PsvxyaVWghhZkC9O6ow_vtW92e4d9lg1ZOWqj9TBN0Bx2xu2uCebZjbAZ2EakcUBwzm1m7M298fYzBhImKTUM0AHKwekZdmUkoTOiB9o9p9zxQUwvLC6805Nyt6WzCfZgvorQ_4RahFpqrd35Lhgxcoo-fUpCLZFq-aSEfFAWasHMfgsk1FJiqm31kSm3hVM-2y_SKgA5gVbddVxNtvtVfQi-mFH08DjRh6_Pfx7ytOo3AAD___-hY-U) to share how they feel about it. Today's poll asks readers to weigh in on Winter Storm Blair. Check it out below! If you suffer from joint pain, be sure to check out today's sponsor [NativePath](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGK8zAQhJ9m3dmsdiXZKVT8YNz85JoErjSyJcUCJTa2Lpe8_aHrr1l2mGH4mM_zeLYxxcftw969ya-wjoSkahQ1dqOzMb1HWTkzoTuJyhvRsmZWXYfVYnCapWrbFqVmS66jbqJZobZ6bqUOqoqmlKHATghGhQ1aoQKf0JEW3gYNEr2Led2bvPjsX_YI6b0-_d7M671KZsl5O4D_AQ1AQ5iah83x6Tebl5IAGnZXTrMtG_BwRAfca6mA9PY1AfckUbUMpGdRFJIsNPV1uIS4H7lmRCwuAff_ZfkYuL8Ol2o3bvuOKcVDtyDxdrcx_VLlP2d6GvoJAAD__3z2Ydk).

## Ready Or Not, Winter Is Coming
Gov. Greg Abbott has [activated state emergency resources](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLtuhDAURL_GdEZ-8CxcpHG3aVOiC76AFYNX9g3e_fuI9GmmGWnm6Hw9pgf44M_tEw409FrjpIRquZBcDJMDH95TUzkzCzfKCo3sdad1Owyi2k23tK7Rcu7VIodxBt251q2D7hvn5DCqypt7TEgxSKlFK2oBsl31KJzqJMLasUag8xRTTTsSviCv4R0vTPUSjyqYneiZmf5gyjJlSyn1dwa6S6bsiSUzZUNcIDBl7yumrJB39EzZLV4c5jkScVjIX0CYeSYg5Hhg2vBc3jxhjj9pwcxhR3A8rrz4kzDxgkA7JqZslYx7Fh-Cz13PGrEd4MMfIf2r7DLqNwAA__93_G4R) in anticipation of snow, freezing rain, and ice that Winter Storm Blair will bring to much of Texas over the next few days. Up to an inch of snow is expected in parts of the Panhandle and West Texas today, while Dallas and Fort Worth could receive [between 3 and 6 inches](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1uhDAQhJ_GdKD1D8YULtLQXdqUyODlsGQwslfH8fYRp7RpZz59Gs3PY3y4EMP-_HYbWnovaRQg2hp4DWb0LsRrVJW3E_ieV2h5J7WUrTFQrbbrJzUvaKScuTBGtLqflPJTv_hOKNlWwd4y4GA4l9BCA463i-zBC83RLZopQB8o5YZWJHy7ssQrvTA3c9qqaFeiozD5xcTAxHCeZ7Ndxe1up7SHdENMDDue5W7R0YqZicFlCnPEOws7Ya4LpbzVH3_9h9UCgBvd6-ZYjypbf5whxlB0xxQ8NxfiZwL9-8nLit8AAAD__25rZDo) tomorrow and Friday. Austin, San Antonio, and the Hill Country could get freezing rain and sleet beginning tomorrow evening. The forecast has caused prices for this weekend’s Cotton Bowl matchup between UT and Ohio State to plummet, with the cheapest tickets dropping from about $500 to [around a third of that price](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00DFv5SAQBOBfgzusZTEYFxTXuMu1V1oYlhiF97DszXPy709On3pGn0bz7215C6WW5_vf8CDPX7ktCGgkKAluSaHU72Xokl8hTaojr0ZttTbOQbd5zDiNiTDhOiBNEc0UMasIakBcDXTF3xgocEppMNBDUCbrCRJaRSFbMQClwu3oeSOmr3Dm-t1edPSxPbrqN-b9FPqPwFngfF1X__H6pDsUOIeDS6wkcD73dvApcH7GEATOP5JsW2ny5MAkucQP4lPuR4kkY2NuT7m2q8rAfHdS-bxJdKO00eZgtJZZuUEOa7TSaXIyaIXkCGwyuTt82q9SazntKAZ4f4RSfzbzrye-PP4PAAD__8_AcqA). The cold weather didn’t keep fans from tailgating during Monday’s [FCS Championship Game](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1uwyAURp8Gb1gX8O_A0MVbuna0buBiI2FjAQ1Jn75K927f8Ono6Hzd1hv64M_tEw_S5eniKkH2HASHabXow2vtGqvvYGfRkBajGpTqpwmaXQORusPcgZon45wxAkc7j8r2d2VGMzdev2EgYBJCQQ8toOidmsHKQRC6gXVA1peY2rJToSdmF17xQak18WiC3ku5MlMfTC5MLrXW1sVnd1LN7wOTy3syuTiTudnxuHw88-4vvuFBvKAPGxZKmbvks4n8-3T4Q5ZXfxZKvBKWnVKTtL2qD8HnYWQdbAf68GdQ_k3y0PI3AAD__x8yZpA) in Frisco between North Dakota State and Montana State, however. Some Texans who lived through the worst of Winter Storm Uri in 2021—which caused prolonged blackouts, burst pipes, and water outages across the state—have been [triggered by the cold snap](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOszAQBOCnMR1obYOBwsXf0OVvr0QbWAdLxkb2Kk7e_pT0V89oPml-busNffDx8R9PsvxyaVWghhZkC9O6ow_vtW92e4d9lg1ZOWqj9TBN0Bx2xu2uCebZjbAZ2EakcUBwzm1m7M298fYzBhImKTUM0AHKwekZdmUkoTOiB9o9p9zxQUwvLC6805Nyt6WzCfZgvorQ_4RahFpqrd35Lhgxcoo-fUpCLZFq-aSEfFAWasHMfgsk1FJiqm31kSm3hVM-2y_SKgA5gVbddVxNtvtVfQi-mFH08DjRh6_Pfx7ytOo3AAD___-hY-U), expressing their distaste for winter on social media. Others have poked fun at the perceived overreaction. "_It's cold, not the end of the world, Texans. We will get through this cold front. Just stay safe and warm. And pray the grid doesn't shut down_ ,” a Reddit user posted.
## SMU Hires President of UT
University of Texas at Austin President Jay Hartzell has been [named the next president of Southern Methodist University](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1uhDAQhJ_GdEbrH_4KF2noLm1KtJzXhyMDJ7x3QJ4-uqRIlXZmNJ_0fVyGC8YUl9s7zuT4COugQVcSlIR28BjTOdjCuxF8pwpyqjG1MVXbQjG5sVNdex29RYSKOhrBgm1g1HVQhIBFdK8zUNAqZaCCElBVwXTgda0IQy0skI-8biVPxHRgDulcn7SV13UukpuY71mYN6F7oft938uwHg0-MsflNRG6X2jPQvcPlr-xvG-Uo6eF5SeecsKNvygludDBMs-Pv77YnL_vMaWY60ZYuM0Y0w-Y_zXxdPo7AAD__27rYyA) following a unanimous vote by SMU’s Board of Trustees on Tuesday. R. Gerald Turner, SMU’s current president, [announced his retirement in August](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kD2PhCAUAH8NdpgHiB8FxTZ2e-2V5q08VxIEA7i69-sve_3Vk0wm832f7ui8C88v3MiUa4mTBKk5CA79ZNH599RU1jzADqIiIzrVKqX7HqrV9HoGpWat5KDbR7PgIJtOdose2kEPWlTOfGQgoBdCgYYaUOhFDWBlKwiXljVA1pWY6rJSoQvz4t_xRame41Z5s5ayZ6ZuTI5Mjud51lsOH8bkSIEfmclxi4HeTI4zJqKUMViyx4zFxcDkeBS-J8rOUih8xVR-yHueC-27C09u4xl4ifxBPG8HzzzQVbgntJSYHDHx201c3YlQJWP303nvctuxBp4bOv-XWf799jLyNwAA__8mknD4) after a 30-year tenure marked by the establishment of the George W. Bush Presidential Center and the school’s transition to the Atlantic Coast Conference. Hartzell has led UT since 2020, [guiding the school](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDHPozAMhn9N2FI5CVAYMtzC9t16I0oTp1gKCSKmLf_-RIfbbrFs69WjR--fn_nHUaL8_O1WtPyJZdagOwlKwjAHR-mc2ybYB4RRNWjV3fTGdMMAzWJN1ytn9KA8QBjaMWLsR4gP30UcfT82ZC8YKBiUMtDBDZzqohkh6F6hi71oAQNx2W-8IOPH1ZjO8sL95svaJLswb1WYX0JPQk--rOuRiU9aN-f5ygg9uaMyZaGnWg5epMfMu0vy3xvD4R1TufZLR-gJ1DXuQk8Hy23HSgEzSy6yMm4ylHe-joQuyC8V9yxX5KUEqiyPTC_cK_Ep9NTsNmxvSolqfxctPFdH6avP_-3zZfXfAAAA__8q_nos) through the challenges of the COVID pandemic, the state-mandated elimination of DEI programs at public universities, and the pro-Palestinian protests that rocked campuses across the country last spring. In a [statement released by SMU](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00DFyqzAQgOHTiE7MSgKMChWvofNrUzKLdzHKSOBBa2Ny-kzS5wL_P_N9XMcrxhTX-3_MHOQ9b6MF22owGvqRMKZzbCoKE5A3FQdzcZ1zbd9DtYTJ-suEiB3dWu8d020yfsK-JTau6aiK4ScGBnpjHLRQA5p2dh7IdoZx7lQDTFG2vZaFhd9Y5nRuL97r25arFBaRR1Hun7KDssNxHHXJz5rpqeyw8lGUHRIKF1F2-MRTL7jLF6ekV8xMeuW36MfOJRKvordZl_ys9kCPI6YUS3dRDdwzxvT7kz8BXsF-BwAA__8Fj19e), Hartzell said he was “ _honored to serve as the next president of an exceptional institution like SMU in one of the most dynamic and fastest-growing cities in the nation, and excited to see how I can make the greatest impact as a leader_.”
## 2,300 Personalized License Plate Requests Rejected
A North Texas TV station obtained a list of the [over 2,300 personalized license plate requests](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGO8yAQBOCnwR3WAsZxCoq_cZe_vdLCsMRcCFhmEyf39Cenv26K0afRfF2mi40p5ut_e0dDr1AmCVJzEByGyduY3lPXeDODP4sGjTipXik9DNAsJsxh6Jw7oz7pXs6oUDsIEGa0rofZNtEcGAgYhFCgoQUrdFBn8LIXaEPPOkAfqWwtLUj4sjWkd3ni1rpyb5JZiNbK1D8mRybHfd_bUF5dxr0eBSbHIzI5bviNjtDzFbdask3xBz1P0WGuyNdkCSv_-PyJW40lc3fL84OIlyzeIVe-dPuNHrbZjF_3mFKs_Yl1cL3bmD5r6M97nkb-BgAA___16Wvr) rejected by the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles last year for being deemed obscene, derogatory, or objectionable. Some of the “best of the worst,” as the station put it, include “CKNBUTT,” “EV SUCKS,” and “R3DNCK,” joining [2023 rejectees](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0z7GO8yAQBOCnwZ2tZcGEFBR_4y5_e6W1gSXmRExkuDh5-5PTXzsafaP5uswXSjmtt_90Z9descwIOPYge7BzoJTfs-6Cu0I4y46dPCmj1GgtdIsb6UxeWs-aEFHSFbXVJo7ReO3lNXbJHRhIsFIqGGEAkmNUZwhoJFM0QgOH1Mo2tIUbv6jG_C5P3gZf7l12S2uPKtQ_gZPAad_3IZYXmqX81FbWoyRwWnmvAqeNv9k3Dv3H6XPyvFbuH5mOMJatR0DVbS489pRzquYkNNzulPJnrf15_-nwNwAA___GgF16) "UR2SLOO," "SRRY KRN," and "JOMAMMA." The department also rejected "H4WKTUA" and 81 other submissions related to that meme. Texans can personalize license plates for a fee between $50 to $570, but they should know their submissions will be scrupulously reviewed by TxDMV personnel to ensure they [adhere to agency guidelines](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00L1uwyAUQOGnwVvQhQsXPDBUlTw1VbaOFr8JKoktG6XJ21fp3hf4dHS-jvPR11Zv509_za4_yjJLkPoA4gB2Tr6256yG5AKkUQzZCYOEqK2F4eIwBq9JEQlNQY4GZSQUwUAsQo4pDNW9MBBghUDQwMELXXCEJElkX4gpyKn2ZeP9knt--L2053LPG4_LdWju0vu6M3xjcmJyMrIo0kUli1kaQlPAHiChCRQhqTHEoJGUpqyLVSTGhEh83xuPRfDNx--Ybi-ZyelY98jkdPo4ze9b7Xmrnq-pDJtL609tre5kmILz1df2F9P_vXN38jcAAP__Sohhuw).
| Flying together with our sponsor [](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGK8zAQhJ9m3dmsdiXZKVT8YNz85JoErjSyJcUCJTa2Lpe8_aHrr1l2mGH4mM_zeLYxxcftw969ya-wjoSkahQ1dqOzMb1HWTkzoTuJyhvRsmZWXYfVYnCapWrbFqVmS66jbqJZobZ6bqUOqoqmlKHATghGhQ1aoQKf0JEW3gYNEr2Led2bvPjsX_YI6b0-_d7M671KZsl5O4D_AQ1AQ5iah83x6Tebl5IAGnZXTrMtG_BwRAfca6mA9PY1AfckUbUMpGdRFJIsNPV1uIS4H7lmRCwuAff_ZfkYuL8Ol2o3bvuOKcVDtyDxdrcx_VLlP2d6GvoJAAD__3z2Ydk) Think your joint pain is just something you have to live with? Think again. Scientists have uncovered [**something remarkable**](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGK8zAQhJ9m3dmsdiXZKVT8YNz85JoErjSyJcUCJTa2Lpe8_aHrr1l2mGH4mM_zeLYxxcftw969ya-wjoSkahQ1dqOzMb1HWTkzoTuJyhvRsmZWXYfVYnCapWrbFqVmS66jbqJZobZ6bqUOqoqmlKHATghGhQ1aoQKf0JEW3gYNEr2Led2bvPjsX_YI6b0-_d7M671KZsl5O4D_AQ1AQ5iah83x6Tebl5IAGnZXTrMtG_BwRAfca6mA9PY1AfckUbUMpGdRFJIsNPV1uIS4H7lmRCwuAff_ZfkYuL8Ol2o3bvuOKcVDtyDxdrcx_VLlP2d6GvoJAAD__3z2Ydk) in the pristine waters of the Antarctic Ocean - a unique form of omega-3 that's showing unprecedented results for joint comfort. Unlike typical fish oils, this rare marine source contains a specialized form of fatty acids that work differently in your body. What's really turning heads? How quickly people are reporting differences in their joint mobility and comfort. Even more fascinating? This powerful compound works with your body's natural systems in ways that ordinary supplements can't match. While most joint solutions just mask the discomfort, this Antarctic discovery gets to the root cause of inflammation. Ready to discover why the coldest waters on Earth might hold the key to your joint comfort? [**What you're about to learn could change everything about how you move**](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGK8zAQhJ9m3dmsdiXZKVT8YNz85JoErjSyJcUCJTa2Lpe8_aHrr1l2mGH4mM_zeLYxxcftw969ya-wjoSkahQ1dqOzMb1HWTkzoTuJyhvRsmZWXYfVYnCapWrbFqVmS66jbqJZobZ6bqUOqoqmlKHATghGhQ1aoQKf0JEW3gYNEr2Led2bvPjsX_YI6b0-_d7M671KZsl5O4D_AQ1AQ5iah83x6Tebl5IAGnZXTrMtG_BwRAfca6mA9PY1AfckUbUMpGdRFJIsNPV1uIS4H7lmRCwuAff_ZfkYuL8Ol2o3bvuOKcVDtyDxdrcx_VLlP2d6GvoJAAD__3z2Ydk).

**➤ Gov. Greg Abbott** named Texas Supreme Court Justice Jimmy Blacklock as the court’s next chief justice following the retirement of former Chief Justice Nathan Hecht. Abbott also appointed his general counsel, James P. Sullivan, to fill the vacant position on the court. ([More](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL3u4yAQxJ8Gd1gL-AMXFNe4y7VXWhiWmAQby2zi5O1PiXTSNf9uNKMZ_TR_LtPFxhS362-7oqFXyJME2XIQHPTkbUzvqam8mcEPokIjetUp1WoN1WKsRkTvtRCucQOg051uBuihGXQYhraK5jMGArQQClqowYo2qAG87ATa0LEG0EfKR00LEr5sCemdn3jULq9VMgvRXpj6xeTI5HieZ73NzofzEzM5bngWJsc9p0jRfWTKG_JC9uD_mdf85HaeMxG3-57jRoXfHoWiQ36L6_rmc7LunrK7c1v4l4OXx37gitzlx0HcLRHDvxKTo-pV0wrJ5Fgdxu9nTCmWrmcNXFcb0xeffvzzaeTfAAAA__--OHqA)) ****➤ Austin:**** Police believe a Saturday morning fire at a homeless camp was an attempt to cover up a murder. An autopsy of the unidentified male victim revealed he died from sharp force injuries. ([More](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0jzFzhCAUhH8NdDgPENSCIo3dpU3pcPI43wzKjZDT-_cZkzrtzs7u933dppunRNvj06_o6hnzpEAZAVJAPwVP6T21PLg7hEFydLLTVmvT98AX16oO-gE6MNIM2qMe7N2DAY_z3bYycHLXGEjopdRgoAEvTdQDBGUl-mhZCxio5r2pC1Y8fYnpnV-4N3NeeXJLrc_C9AdTI1PjcRxNzGfnv0ul7aowNW54FKbGv0xE2ksVS15ppoAiR3ER8N2F50EpUbEda-Gxekq_F_Vf55dTPwEAAP__519Ydw)) **➤ Corpus Christi:** A roll of the dice determined the winner of the District 1 election for city council. Incumbent Everett Roy was declared the winner over Billy Lerma after the two candidates remained tied following a recount of last month’s runoff election. ([More](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2O2zAQhJ-G6tbgj6TjFSzSqLu0KQ2KXFmL0KRBrsXT2wcK0qYczDcDzPz6un95SpQfP_0THX9v5a6lnkAqkPYePaXzPg7RrTJ-qgGd-jCzMZO1ctidtdpas0Yf4-THSUWjVQzGmHWzYcR5IHeVSSWtUkZO8ia9mjbzKaOeFfptFqPESFzqjXdk_PZtS2c5sN5CeQ7J7cyvJswPoRehl9777XelxsdlC71k7E3oJZXgE_wTlOEs7woZ6bGvpe6lRKGXUOrr3SDsV56EXmLpmUvPQi8-R-AdoVPOWIEaUA7v54qZAQ-syAy1nNApJQglM-U3Aheo-KrYLixSY1BDdfF1UdTmDzHKx9NT-juF__vt4fSfAAAA__9k8YD2)) **➤ Arlington:** The city has contracted with Lewisville-based Open Space Development to deploy a herd of more than 400 goats to clear invasive Chinese Privet from a local park. ([See Photos](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO6yAURL8Gd1gXbMAuKF7jLq_d0rqBS4KWGAuQnfz9yttvN8VoNOd83dYbxhS3x398kW3vkFcJUnEQHKbVY0yfdey8vYOfRUdWmEEPg5om6J6WaNQgzOwMKR2Cd8ppZcykNNF8l_cu2msMBExCDKCgBxQqDDN4qQVh0GwE8rHl0rcnNXpjDemTDyq9y68u2Wdre2XDPyYXJpfzPPuQ3-NGZ70KTC5XZHIZAfgjY6vcJcLC43ZgjQfxPeHWeN3JRapcAPf4qRzLhdzyxncs312xfj9jSrFqw0Z4vDCm3wPtTyOHlT8BAAD__wguZII)) **➤ Houston:** Rapper and Grapevine native Post Malone gave a waitress at the bar he visited on Christmas Eve a $20,000 tip. Malone performed with Beyoncé on Christmas Day during the halftime show of the Texans-Ravens game at NRG Stadium. clean_body: A morning newsletter focused entirely on news from the state of Texas. If it’s important news inside TX state lines, we cover it. | | | | | | Wednesday, January 8, 2025 ---|--- Good morning! On this day in 1919, ballerina was born in El Paso. She made her first professional appearance in Los Angeles at the age of 14, and she later became the first American to gain prima ballerina roles in a European company, performing in classical ballets such as Giselle and Swan Lake. The winter weather is coming, and Texans are to share how they feel about it. Today's poll asks readers to weigh in on Winter Storm Blair. Check it out below! If you suffer from joint pain, be sure to check out today's sponsor . Abilene ❄️ 38/24° | Amarillo ⛅ 38/18° | Austin ☁️ 43/31° | Brownsville 🌧️ 58/46° | Corpus Christi ☁️ 48/42° | DFW ⛅ 41/26° | El Paso ❄️ 36/29° | Houston ☁️ 45/35° | Laredo ☁️ 47/39° | Lubbock ☁️ 38/21° | Midland ❄️ 35/21° | San Antonio ☁️ 41/35° | Tyler ⛅ 43/26° | Waco 🌥️ 42/26° | Ready Or Not, Winter Is Coming Gov. Greg Abbott has in anticipation of snow, freezing rain, and ice that Winter Storm Blair will bring to much of Texas over the next few days. Up to an inch of snow is expected in parts of the Panhandle and West Texas today, while Dallas and Fort Worth could receive tomorrow and Friday. Austin, San Antonio, and the Hill Country could get freezing rain and sleet beginning tomorrow evening. The forecast has caused prices for this weekend’s Cotton Bowl matchup between UT and Ohio State to plummet, with the cheapest tickets dropping from about $500 to . The cold weather didn’t keep fans from tailgating during Monday’s in Frisco between North Dakota State and Montana State, however. Some Texans who lived through the worst of Winter Storm Uri in 2021—which caused prolonged blackouts, burst pipes, and water outages across the state—have been , expressing their distaste for winter on social media. Others have poked fun at the perceived overreaction. "It's cold, not the end of the world, Texans. We will get through this cold front. Just stay safe and warm. And pray the grid doesn't shut down ,” a Reddit user posted. SMU Hires President of UT University of Texas at Austin President Jay Hartzell has been following a unanimous vote by SMU’s Board of Trustees on Tuesday. R. Gerald Turner, SMU’s current president, after a 30-year tenure marked by the establishment of the George W. Bush Presidential Center and the school’s transition to the Atlantic Coast Conference. Hartzell has led UT since 2020, through the challenges of the COVID pandemic, the state-mandated elimination of DEI programs at public universities, and the pro-Palestinian protests that rocked campuses across the country last spring. In a , Hartzell said he was “ honored to serve as the next president of an exceptional institution like SMU in one of the most dynamic and fastest-growing cities in the nation, and excited to see how I can make the greatest impact as a leader.” 2,300 Personalized License Plate Requests Rejected A North Texas TV station obtained a list of the rejected by the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles last year for being deemed obscene, derogatory, or objectionable. Some of the “best of the worst,” as the station put it, include “CKNBUTT,” “EV SUCKS,” and “R3DNCK,” joining "UR2SLOO," "SRRY KRN," and "JOMAMMA." The department also rejected "H4WKTUA" and 81 other submissions related to that meme. Texans can personalize license plates for a fee between $50 to $570, but they should know their submissions will be scrupulously reviewed by TxDMV personnel to ensure they . | Flying together with our sponsor Think your joint pain is just something you have to live with? Think again. Scientists have uncovered in the pristine waters of the Antarctic Ocean - a unique form of omega-3 that's showing unprecedented results for joint comfort. Unlike typical fish oils, this rare marine source contains a specialized form of fatty acids that work differently in your body. What's really turning heads? How quickly people are reporting differences in their joint mobility and comfort. Even more fascinating? This powerful compound works with your body's natural systems in ways that ordinary supplements can't match. While most joint solutions just mask the discomfort, this Antarctic discovery gets to the root cause of inflammation. Ready to discover why the coldest waters on Earth might hold the key to your joint comfort? . | ➤ Gov. Greg Abbott named Texas Supreme Court Justice Jimmy Blacklock as the court’s next chief justice following the retirement of former Chief Justice Nathan Hecht. Abbott also appointed his general counsel, James P. Sullivan, to fill the vacant position on the court. () ➤ Austin: Police believe a Saturday morning fire at a homeless camp was an attempt to cover up a murder. An autopsy of the unidentified male victim revealed he died from sharp force injuries. () ➤ Corpus Christi: A roll of the dice determined the winner of the District 1 election for city council. Incumbent Everett Roy was declared the winner over Billy Lerma after the two candidates remained tied following a recount of last month’s runoff election. () ➤ Arlington: The city has contracted with Lewisville-based Open Space Development to deploy a herd of more than 400 goats to clear invasive Chinese Privet from a local park. () ➤ Houston: Rapper and Grapevine native Post Malone gave a waitress at the bar he visited on Christmas Eve a $20,000 tip. Malone performed with Beyoncé on Christmas Day during the halftime show of the Texans-Ravens game at NRG Stadium. () ➤ Austin: An anonymous tip led to the recovery of a food truck stolen the morning after Christmas. Roy Lotz, owner of Lotz of BBQ, plans to use the $33,000 he received from a GoFundMe campaign to repair the trailer, which was heavily damaged and stripped of its components. () ➤ Dallas Mavericks guard Kyrie Irving is expected to miss one to two weeks with a bulging disc in his back. With Luka Doncic sidelined due to a calf strain, the team's top two scorers are out with injuries. () ➤ Texas A &M entered the top 10 in the most recent AP Top 25 men's basketball poll, while Houston also continued to climb up the rankings, rising two spots to No. 12. () ➤ Former Dallas Cowboys running back Ezekiel Elliott was signed to the Los Angeles Chargers' practice squad ahead of the team's Saturday wild card matchup against the Houston Texans. Donald Trump Jr. was recently appointed as an adviser to the company and will become an equity holder upon the deal’s completion. name: Amy Carnevale
subject: Taxpayer-funded guns & fentanyl ?
body:
MA Republicans: END STATE-FUNDED CRIME NOW!
| | | | | [](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0012ETjHQSLflX3mD6Xn8f3sy-lS4Ld-ZJGLNXQrkFKn7AAlG8BdcF4jh7ghVrLu10P5oboUeC4vSGq6P5RWqFHLjc5tX_TPo2_tMoLn4KHwXkWnsSn0yGJBwF4DvSbIMkXEUuugGVem5mU7AgyytLWow==&c=AUepe4svYFNdprZjERICmQ54gNHKSy5jIKmdXRK6CRYAkv_PHa2xWQ==&ch=QixbPmqHIakBbaKY3jet89lrBGTu-C_eZUDZk63q5a6Ee9XX0k9EGQ==)
---
| Dear Friends,
Warning ⚠️ another day, another horrific story of illegal immigrant crime in the news... ⚠️
This time, an undocumented immigrant was arrested for allegedly having a high capacity firearm, cocaine, $1,000,000 worth of fentanyl, and a flourishing drug enterprise, all run from the comforts of a state-funded migrant shelter in Revere.
Our Republican Caucus is all over this story and is demanding accountability from the Healey-Driscoll Administration.
How is it possible an undocumented immigrant here illegally ended up in an emergency family shelter even though our laws do not allow it?
How did he pass the background check as part of the application process?
How did he gain access to a high capability firearm despite our strict gun laws?
How was a criminal drug enterprise being run out of a taxpayer-funded family shelter?
How is the Healey-Driscoll administration letting this happen to Massachusetts?
What will it take to get her to stand up for us?
---
| | [TAKE OUR POLL: SHOULD GOV. HEALEY AMEND THE RIGHT TO SHELTER LAW?](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0012ETjHQSLflX3mD6Xn8f3sy-lS4Ld-ZJGLNXQrkFKn7AAlG8BdcF4jikl6gjjwmP7EKm4sZVcRJFTxF0e-XZ2z-FThIQOg5o77ybGARPxJHpNWqAfq5mbX5T34xGbrrQ8BhGgZcjpupZpmN-yR0HttqaGLIhehxt6Fm_2RKnlT_T5M2TBBhiELjEuuwba2jTX&c=AUepe4svYFNdprZjERICmQ54gNHKSy5jIKmdXRK6CRYAkv_PHa2xWQ==&ch=QixbPmqHIakBbaKY3jet89lrBGTu-C_eZUDZk63q5a6Ee9XX0k9EGQ==)
---
| Massachusetts Republicans stand strong in our call to amend the Right to Shelter Law residency requirements.
The migrant crisis has spiraled into a financial catastrophe, a humanitarian disaster, and a severe threat to public safety.
Massachusetts residents deserve answers and the Democratic Supermajority needs to put partisanship aside and work with Republicans on solutions.
---
| | [TAKE OUR POLL: MAKE YOUR STANCE KNOWN](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0012ETjHQSLflX3mD6Xn8f3sy-lS4Ld-ZJGLNXQrkFKn7AAlG8BdcF4jikl6gjjwmP7EKm4sZVcRJFTxF0e-XZ2z-FThIQOg5o77ybGARPxJHpNWqAfq5mbX5T34xGbrrQ8BhGgZcjpupZpmN-yR0HttqaGLIhehxt6Fm_2RKnlT_T5M2TBBhiELjEuuwba2jTX&c=AUepe4svYFNdprZjERICmQ54gNHKSy5jIKmdXRK6CRYAkv_PHa2xWQ==&ch=QixbPmqHIakBbaKY3jet89lrBGTu-C_eZUDZk63q5a6Ee9XX0k9EGQ==)

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1790, President George Washington delivered [the first State of the Union](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGu4yAQheGnwR0WDA6xC4pt3GXbLS0SBjMrAhHMxvLbr3J121t_0tHR_-e23TxlKvtv_0THsW6g4CKVlmregqd8btMQXEQENAM6fTXWGKvneUhusWj8cr8qUMvkw4LxbkzU-hGWS4T7PJD7rCmtZq2NBT16a-3yCBG00hABxaQwENc2_q1UOGHMZ31jGx_1OWSXmF9dmF8CVgHrcRxjos61nR8XsHKiLoM_JRX5LQLWV8NOAQvLHWvbUR6-Jyo71yIDZnpj6zJS6yw7e0ZZo-SE8l-hWobmwuugnKnbq5jU_vSUv-7wT3neDv4HAAD__84SarM) address to the Congress in New York City. ## Judge Blocks Release of Trump Report
A federal judge temporarily blocked the [release of special counsel Jack Smith's report](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO6yAQRb_G7ohgwJgUFK9Jl9duaY1hiPGCsTBJlL9fefstr650dHS-7tMdY4rb4z9msi2UCTgMjAvGzeQxps-kem8DEZDsyYpRaim1MKZfLGnpDUIYrsMccCa6DgoDDTMfAAXJPtqTxgU3QkgN4oJa66vzAQQXEIA6xcnHVuplLXFrC4X0KS-qF1dyn-zS2n508l8Htw5uuG_0Ps7rHLVFl6iDW6vPvLMV3Tc7cmwLy1gx4aOwFTem2fo8WnTEPO1YW6atMRolKpi5dE57Z_joSYJT4J0BFaTvq_X7O6YUDz12ij8yxvTr1P5q9LLwEwAA__8_7mkd) on investigations into President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday.

The two-volume report is expected to [detail Smith's charging decisions](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00DGP4yAUBOBfgzsseGBiFxTXuMu1V1oYnpOXw2ABiZV_v8puvfWnGY3m33W5OoqUbn_djrZteQEBAxeSi3EJjuJ70V2wGyKg6tDKizJKGTmO3d1epDJ6GtbBr2GEQYcJxCYN4KSDxkl3ZD9tQopRSmVA9s4YM_mwgRQSNkCmBQZqufSPTKndcYvv_MLS-7x30d5bOypTfxjMDObzPPu0-oRn_TiD-ciRGvnKYA45uRh4K8_9YDA_nuGGfI3Z_6-8YERXkdcDPbnIfX6mipEXPHJpP5nKfaGd0oddxcqLT06OxgjdFRuOk2Kkai5Mi9vuKH4vbL899rLwFQAA__8Nd2_V) in both the classified documents case at Mar-a-Lago and Trump's alleged involvement in the events of Jan. 6, 2021.
## Meta Ends Political Fact-Checking
Meta will end its [third-party fact-checking program](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGPhCAUhH8Ndhh4KLoFxTV2e-2V5gkP5UTZILdm79dfvH67yUwyX_J93cc7hhj2-RM3MsWnEQS0XEgu-tFhiK-xqZzxRECqIiM7pZXSsu-rxdCtban1gI3qlJvITb1zsp8QOq2goyqY601I0UupNMgatdY36zxIIcEDsUaQCyXl-juFvSzk4ys9Kdc2bVU0SymPg6kPBgOD4TzP2k7HTudx7QyGKzIYNirIPVqaUlp52I-Cc8btqgq3C9k17DPfMK_898eulCfKM4OhysY9zhBjOHTHGjFvGOI_urxT8TTwFwAA__9gamJR) on Facebook, Instagram, and Threads, replacing it with a "Community Notes" system similar to X's model. The company plans to shift its content moderation teams [from liberal California to conservative Texas](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuhDAQBb_GdEbeBXykcJGG7tKmRMZejk1sfILNIf4-In2a14w0eprP-3j3nHh9fPhMTuYyosFOG9CmH6PndI5tFd1MhNRU5ODW2Kax0PfV4hqcIHaAwXcQ0Ydo286bqQvB-hkmqthdNgOmB2gsQu2ttW8hzggGcEZSraHIUrb6q_AqC83pLC_a6lByldwi8txV865wUDgcx1GHdQoXVDhcaoWDgWtuCodM4jUlzrx6oV3LwlvUT7_JqWcfRIeFwjevD53L6-JFh5Lzz8py6rUI7fUiOVWbi8-DU-Ld3lRrHtlz-jsk_wV6OfwNAAD__1B3aLQ) while maintaining oversight of illegal content involving drugs, terrorism, and child exploitation. The changes, announced as Meta appointed Trump ally and UFC chief Dana White to its board, represent the latest in a series of moves repositioning the company politically, including a $1 million donation to President-elect Donald Trump's inaugural fund. Watch [Mark Zuckerberg explain the new policy here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z71uwyAUQOGngS0W9_JjGBi6eEvXjhYxl5gGh8imttKnr9I9-9Enna_zeA655Pv1MyzkW6ojCtQnASdhxxhyeY6KR5-IkCQnD700Uhqwls_-0qOK5KLsIQaNCrRyQQJYctomhzz7lyZAWABpELpgjHFTTAgCMCExJSjmVtfuu-Z7mymVZ91p7aa68OLn1h4bkx8MB4bDcRxdChNdar29AobD7890YzjsOVLdGA6gUUuLTisrwEngq4-PI5eSN9MzJa5LyOUfb-9md49_AQAA__-aM1MI).
## Fast-Moving Fire Rages in California
A wildfire erupted in Southern California Tuesday, quickly [burning 1,200 acres](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFu7CAQRb8Gd1gw-GG7oHiNu02b0pqFwZ6IBcuQtfbvo02f9l7p6Oh83tYbcuK8feCDXItlBQX_pNJSTWtATq916IKLRECmI6dHY42xepq63YX7MAHYYOzdm9mHKYQ4As7Gkx5xHjp2b5rSatLaWNA9WmtnHyJopSECiUFR4FbO_qtwbjvF9CpPOntfHl1ye2tHFea_gEXAcl1X7-8101Xfv4AllYp5o0RVwPLeBSwHeo7s5YGJKwaqMvJJcudtlxfnUGUt322nM0uPiWM5M6OApTtdOC5OiasdxaC2B3L69Wh_dXk6-AkAAP__rvFnPQ) and triggering the evacuation of 30,000 people throughout parts of Los Angeles and Ventura counties, including the Pacific Palisades. name: Dominic Rapini
subject: A New Years Resolution for Connecticut Elections
body:
Let's resolve to bring trust to our elections

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us16.campaign-archive.com/?e=8763c56857&u=ebcefd906ec01db66e79c886c&id=a4f5267346)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| (**ACTION** : Forward this email to your local lawmakers)
---
|
---
| | | | | “Voting is the most precious right of every citizen, and we have a moral obligation to ensure the integrity of our voting process.” — Hillary Clinton
_**“Sorry, I couldn’t resist. Now we know why Hillary Clinton**_ _**won the Presidential Medal of Freedom.”**_
---
|
---
| **Securing the Vote:**
**A New Year’s Resolution for Trustworthy Connecticut Elections**
---
| | | | | | | | | |
---
| _By Dominic Rapini, American Businessman and Political Commentator_
As a proud, principled Republican and former candidate for Connecticut Secretary of State, my New Year’s resolution is clear: it’s time to restore trust in our electoral process through common-sense measures. Elections should reflect the people's will, not become a tug-of-war over loopholes and gray areas. Connecticut deserves better, and here’s how we can achieve that.
**IDs: The Key to Trust**
Let’s start with the basics. In a country where you need an ID to rent a car, buy a six-pack, or even enter some office buildings, why on earth don’t we require one to vote? Requiring government-issued identification to vote isn’t controversial—it’s common sense. 87% of Americans support voter ID laws.
But wait, there’s more! Starting May 9, 2025, the federal Real ID—that fancy one with the gold star—becomes the standard for accessing government buildings, courthouses, air travel, and even crossing borders. It’s like the new golden ticket, except instead of getting into Willy Wonka’s chocolate factory, it gets you through TSA. This isn’t just about elections; it’s about being a functioning citizen in the United States.
Connecticut should get ahead by helping citizens secure their Real IDs. Let’s make this a seamless part of the voting process. Picture this: a voter walks into town hall, flashes their Real ID (or gathers the necessary documents to get one), and walks out ready to vote and navigate the bureaucratic maze of modern life. It’s like killing two birds with one gold star.
Whether you’re voting in person or requesting an absentee ballot, a simple ID check can go a long way toward rebuilding confidence in the system. Shouldn’t ballot casting carry the same standard if IDs are required for everyday tasks? After all, no one wants their elections to be less secure than their Netflix account.
**Drop Boxes: Safe, Secure, and Sensible**
Ballot drop boxes were a pragmatic solution during the height of the pandemic, but their security remains a weak link in the chain. Leaving them unattended on sidewalks or street corners is an open invitation for misuse or tampering.
The solution is straightforward: move drop boxes indoors to government buildings, where they can be monitored under video surveillance. Access should be limited to business hours, just as you’d expect when paying taxes or renewing your driver’s license. This isn’t about making voting harder; it’s about making it more secure.
**Fixing the Frenzy of Same-Day Registration**
I’ve seen firsthand how chaotic same-day registration can be. On Election Day, registrars are expected to verify newly registered voters while simultaneously running a high-stakes election. It’s like drinking from a fire hose—and the result is often rushed, error-prone vetting that undermines the integrity of the process.
Here’s a compromise: Any same-day or Election Day registrations that cannot be fully vetted in time should be treated as provisional ballots. Once the voter's information is verified, these ballots can be counted in full. This ensures no legitimate voter is turned away but guarantees that shortcuts won’t compromise the system. We can’t afford to undermine confidence in our elections by rushing through this critical process.
Let’s be honest: voters have 364 days to register, so waiting until the last minute doesn’t look good. Encouraging procrastination only adds to the chaos, which the current system invites. Worse yet, we shouldn’t see busloads of college students arriving at polling stations at the eleventh hour, clutching boxes of pizza and an agenda they’re only halfway invested in. Voting should reflect a genuine connection to the local community—not just a rushed stop on the way back to campus.
We should also address situations where individuals who don’t meet Connecticut’s residency or citizenship requirements still want to vote. For example, a voter who just moved here and failed to cast an absentee ballot in their former hometown could be issued a federal ballot. This would allow them to vote for federal offices—like the president or congressional representatives—without participating in state or local elections where they have no meaningful connection. Local races should be decided by residents who understand and are invested in the community—not by “carpetbagger” voters passing through.
By treating unvetted registrations as provisional ballots, encouraging timely registration, and creating a solution for out-of-state voters, we can bring much-needed order and integrity to Connecticut’s same-day registration process. It’s not about making voting harder; it’s about making it fair, secure, and grounded in the community.
**Residency Requirements: The Georgia Gold Standard**
Georgia has emerged as a model for election integrity. It requires new residents to live in the state for at least 30ing. Connecticut should adopt a similar rule. New voters should be required to prove their residency with documentation such as days before vot a utility bill, lease agreement, or updated driver’s license.
You shouldn't be voting here if you’re not invested enough in Connecticut to establish residency. It’s as simple as that.
**One Voter, One Vote: Leave Ranked-Choice Voting Behind**
Ranked-choice voting (RCV) might sound trendy, but it’s a solution in search of a problem. This convoluted system confuses voters, dilutes results, and undermines confidence in elections. Worse, it can leave voters feeling like their ballots didn’t count.
Our democracy is built on a simple principle: one voter, one vote. Why complicate it? Let’s keep Connecticut’s elections straightforward and accessible.
**Hands Off Absentee Ballots: The Voter’s Responsibility**
Absentee voting is an essential option for many voters, and Connecticut has made it easy for everyone to access this convenience. Voters can request an absentee ballot online through the Secretary of State’s website or visit their local town clerk to make a request—hopefully while presenting a government-issued ID. These systems are designed to ensure accessibility, and that’s a good thing.
But accessibility comes with responsibility. While we want to make voting easy, we also need to emphasize the importance of accountability in the process. Voters must take ownership of their ballots, whether they’re voting in person or remotely.
Let’s face it: voting in person is always the safest way to ensure your vote is counted. In-person voting eliminates risks like insufficient signatures, ballots lost in the mail, or outright malfeasance. Even bad luck—such as your late ballot—can undermine your voice when you vote by mail. Once your ballot leaves your hands and enters the postal system, you lose control over its journey.
That’s why voters who can should prioritize casting their ballots in person. For those who must vote absentee, the process must remain secure and free from interference. Political operatives should never be allowed to touch an absentee ballot or application—it’s just too easy for trust to be broken when accountability isn’t clear.
Ultimately, whether voting in person or by mail, the goal is to ensure every legitimate vote is counted and every voter’s voice is heard.
**Voter Fraud: Zero Tolerance**
Voter fraud strikes at the heart of democracy, yet Connecticut’s response has been tepid at best. Every recent fraud case in our state has involved absentee ballots, which are typically mishandled by campaign operatives. This isn’t just illegal; it’s an affront to every honest voter.
To deter future fraud, we need stiff penalties: mandatory jail time for offenders and a lifetime ban on participating in campaigns or running for office. Let's make it clear that you don't get a second chance if you break the public's trust.
**A Trustworthy Future**
None of these proposals are radical; they’re rooted in common sense. By implementing these measures, Connecticut can create a system where voters—regardless of party affiliation—feel confident that their voices are heard and their votes are secure.
Let 2025 be the year we restore trust in our elections. It’s not just a resolution—it’s a responsibility.
---
|
---

name: History Facts
subject: Did cowboys really wear cowboy hats?
body:
Conjuring the traditional image of Wild West cowboys and outlaws is easy. [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68cfpn4lhz2vymzes3fidpups0acbol3f072ase3drxg98htjmq7fcct9tin1oc4u3xvisd8bzeofgmvursxl1o5z06nv4pi5ufkcdneraif9spk17qh6d33ngu6yl10bvzwqvrfkqjz92qx6&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sprqyv&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
# [Old West cowboys and outlaws preferred bowler hats.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sprqyv&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [U.S. History](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sprqyv&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sprqyv&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| C onjuring the traditional image of Wild West cowboys and outlaws is easy. Typically, they’re shown in leather chaps, a vest, and denim pants, accompanied by boots, spurs, and holsters. Most importantly? There’s a cowboy hat: a tall, wide-brimmed head-covering that’s become synonymous with gun-slinging legends such as Billy the Kid, Buffalo Bill, or Hollywood’s John Wayne. Yet surprisingly, many cowboys and outlaws in the Old West (around 1865 to 1900) actually [preferred bowler hats](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sprqyv&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com). The casual counterpart to the more formal derby hat, the bowler was designed by London hatmakers and commissioned by English nobility. Domed and hardened by shellac, the felt cap was reliable and sturdy; according to some accounts, it was designed for gamekeepers who kept losing their top hats to low-hanging branches.
---
A world away from lush English estates, the bowler became a natural choice for life in the American West: The hats didn’t fly off in the wind, their durability withstood the elements, and they could be worn for almost any occasion. Favored by the likes of gambler and gunslinger Bat Masterson and the outlaws known as Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid, the bowler hat maintained its hold on the Western frontier until the famous Stetson took over as the hat of choice. With a wider brim to help block the sun — and the boost Buffalo Bill Cody gave the hat after wearing it in his Wild West shows — the “Boss of the Plains” solidly replaced its British predecessor by the end of the 19th century, and became the hat commonly associated with the Old West today.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sprqyv&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sprqyv&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## By the Numbers
| | Bowler hats produced by Lock & Co. during the height of the trend
---
70,000
| | Amount a cowboy paid for the first Stetson, according to legend
---

| Amount a cowboy paid for the first Stetson, according to legend
---
$5
| | Year Buffalo Bill’s Wild West Show opened
---
1883
| | Average height (in inches) of a traditional bowler hat
---
5-7
| Average height (in inches) of a traditional bowler hat

5-7
| | | Did you know?
---|---|---
## The Stetson myth was called “one of the greatest hoaxes of all time.”
The myth that Old West cowboys primarily wore Stetson hats was first debunked [in 1957 by journalist Lucius Beebe](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sprqyv&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com), who examined thousands of photographs and discovered the prevalence of bowler hats on the Western frontier in the 19th century. He was then inspired to write “The Hat That Won the West,” an op-ed for Salt Lake City’s _Deseret News_ that exposed the myth that the Stetson was always the most popular cowboy hat. He wrote that “a mass delusion” took hold of the American consciousness as a result of “faith and folk-legend,” and even went so far as to call the Stetson myth “one of the greatest hoaxes of all time.”

Typically, they’re shown in leather chaps, a vest, and denim pants, accompanied by boots, spurs, and holsters. Most importantly? There’s a cowboy hat: a tall, wide-brimmed head-covering that’s become synonymous with gun-slinging legends such as Billy the Kid, Buffalo Bill, or Hollywood’s John Wayne. Yet surprisingly, many cowboys and outlaws in the Old West (around 1865 to 1900) actually . The casual counterpart to the more formal derby hat, the bowler was designed by London hatmakers and commissioned by English nobility. Domed and hardened by shellac, the felt cap was reliable and sturdy; according to some accounts, it was designed for gamekeepers who kept losing their top hats to low-hanging branches. A world away from lush English estates, the bowler became a natural choice for life in the American West: The hats didn’t fly off in the wind, their durability withstood the elements, and they could be worn for almost any occasion. Favored by the likes of gambler and gunslinger Bat Masterson and the outlaws known as Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid, the bowler hat maintained its hold on the Western frontier until the famous Stetson took over as the hat of choice. With a wider brim to help block the sun — and the boost Buffalo Bill Cody gave the hat after wearing it in his Wild West shows — the “Boss of the Plains” solidly replaced its British predecessor by the end of the 19th century, and became the hat commonly associated with the Old West today. | Thank you for supporting our advertisers! | By the Numbers | | Bowler hats produced by Lock & Co. during the height of the trend 70,000 | | Amount a cowboy paid for the first Stetson, according to legend $5 | Amount a cowboy paid for the first Stetson, according to legend $5 | | Year Buffalo Bill’s Wild West Show opened 1883 | | Average height (in inches) of a traditional bowler hat 5-7 | Average height (in inches) of a traditional bowler hat 5-7 | | | Did you know? ---|---|--- The Stetson myth was called “one of the greatest hoaxes of all time.” The myth that Old West cowboys primarily wore Stetson hats was first debunked , who examined thousands of photographs and discovered the prevalence of bowler hats on the Western frontier in the 19th century. He was then inspired to write “The Hat That Won the West,” an op-ed for Salt Lake City’s Deseret News that exposed the myth that the Stetson was always the most popular cowboy hat. He wrote that “a mass delusion” took hold of the American consciousness as a result of “faith and folk-legend,” and even went so far as to call the Stetson myth “one of the greatest hoaxes of all time.” Thank you for supporting our advertisers! | Recommended Reading | | | ---|---|--- | | | ---|---|--- | | | ---|--- | ---|--- A daily digest of history's most fascinating happenings. | Want to unsubscribe? | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- Copyright © 2024 History Facts. All rights reserved. 700 N Colorado Blvd, #513, Denver, CO 80206 | Advertisements: | | | | • | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | | | | ---|---|---|---|---

subject: Hearing aids just got their biggest upgrade...
body:
Why this tiny German hearing aid is taking the U.S. by storm, according to the experts

| [ ](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c8c996b7a3c34d6babccd68cc6d90ff2&_e=cD_fCRaxny6ExYp8PO1FSL4RRUTh5orPkz8Y0QBEOaS0XnKjgei2q__vjF8VxqYN86JxAYCBKPKF33-fHwUmktKB_uPboRodPxGFYMqywNGVickcXXNeBepkdeNpQPMA5NBTB6YqsWPuiG_7xscu2qRrXiM1qdMJ729f59oWIDjcCCMhnSIjkeAPCZXecAN166TiSeGifTpBUUQfzCoTW1JAZt91n9tRkcCSMLon5HFFdfysW32ohULEzVT48W9kuSDxsftysK99RdmrQBDf0vhdU9Rrh0A5SO15PbILFHUOluu7T_7Z1k1YMHBEyVkb8Y3FgqJBzcSpwcsvrSsHf_7RdGHm7gCugIsjSOD_msYE3a9IIHq6MHJ_YjEvThU2XOsgoRGEiP-OEIL94WPdsJ_D4E42TDgwsefjsHJTzcF-304xGpXn7EEPXgQU_XZeSDew2SzZSV1RF3VZn0bF97K_wC4uVmUKg-FUCejzKUQ%3D)

| Why this tiny German hearing aid is taking the U.S. by storm, according to the experts
---
[ ](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c8c996b7a3c34d6babccd68cc6d90ff2&_e=cD_fCRaxny6ExYp8PO1FSL4RRUTh5orPkz8Y0QBEOaS0XnKjgei2q__vjF8VxqYN86JxAYCBKPKF33-fHwUmktKB_uPboRodPxGFYMqywNGVickcXXNeBepkdeNpQPMA5NBTB6YqsWPuiG_7xscu2qRrXiM1qdMJ729f59oWIDjcCCMhnSIjkeAPCZXecAN166TiSeGifTpBUUQfzCoTW1JAZt91n9tRkcCSMLon5HFFdfysW32ohULEzVT48W9kuSDxsftysK99RdmrQBDf0vhdU9Rrh0A5SO15PbILFHUOluu7T_7Z1k1YMHBEyVkb8Y3FgqJBzcSpwcsvrSsHf0db_M5HIs8ESJjGOeqzKiB0M6GcLTGTSMDoBkU--fSeLyOrYghdRBxJHWBxupyvN6l0uZFtIOggbGz8rUsVCCQJl92by7-TmT8s2rbjxDP3o7npvBHdoKGJrpl8WNlhjUOCf62oHC6sRcBbkMWYnn8%3D)
**Alarming fact: More than 48 million Americans hear so poorly that their quality of life significantly suffers as a result.**

The problem: Most wait too long to act, hoping their hearing will improve on its own. Sadly, it never does.

But now, **a game-changing device is revolutionizing the hearing aid industry,** and experts say it's the biggest breakthrough they've seen in over a decade.

It's [hear.com's](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c8c996b7a3c34d6babccd68cc6d90ff2&_e=cD_fCRaxny6ExYp8PO1FSL4RRUTh5orPkz8Y0QBEOaS0XnKjgei2q__vjF8VxqYN86JxAYCBKPKF33-fHwUmktKB_uPboRodPxGFYMqywNGVickcXXNeBepkdeNpQPMA5NBTB6YqsWPuiG_7xscu2qRrXiM1qdMJ729f59oWIDjcCCMhnSIjkeAPCZXecAN166TiSeGifTpBUUQfzCoTW1JAZt91n9tRkcCSMLon5HFFdfysW32ohULEzVT48W9kuSDxsftysK99RdmrQBDf0vhdU9Rrh0A5SO15PbILFHUOluu7T_7Z1k1YMHBEyVkb8Y3FgqJBzcSpwcsvrSsHf2lzHHnRn-mVqZU93TKPini2XGHNXi8IT5BcWTFPQD4ksILSDgi41QhufoECYY2_9167tH8KH1S-6InANoB3_2O4tzNRH60KmpnP0EuBrQob0PsD6P1JkOfNjkhnvKFGjWyVdUXmV6jCAN0zX0k4WNI%3D) **new IX hearing aid.**

And it’s the perfect solution at the perfect time—when more and more people are eager to maintain their quality of life and overcome these limitations.

[ ](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c8c996b7a3c34d6babccd68cc6d90ff2&_e=cD_fCRaxny6ExYp8PO1FSL4RRUTh5orPkz8Y0QBEOaS0XnKjgei2q__vjF8VxqYN86JxAYCBKPKF33-fHwUmktKB_uPboRodPxGFYMqywNGVickcXXNeBepkdeNpQPMA5NBTB6YqsWPuiG_7xscu2qRrXiM1qdMJ729f59oWIDjcCCMhnSIjkeAPCZXecAN166TiSeGifTpBUUQfzCoTW1JAZt91n9tRkcCSMLon5HFFdfysW32ohULEzVT48W9kuSDxsftysK99RdmrQBDf0vhdU9Rrh0A5SO15PbILFHUOluu7T_7Z1k1YMHBEyVkb8Y3FgqJBzcSpwcsvrSsHf7VDukCOQ7Ib9nvTRt5kmkPQ1Dft0eGJ8YRY1dIzjIUhV8rCLKPT4NP76JJUz3PHPbg-PB28GxsSeYNPpriL9tD8tj1V_HO1WIPFu_BTplE3xig8kvmqJe0ZZAhRnATZXkvQhF1wIfjvyPz05DkGWc8%3D)
What makes [hear.com's](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c8c996b7a3c34d6babccd68cc6d90ff2&_e=cD_fCRaxny6ExYp8PO1FSL4RRUTh5orPkz8Y0QBEOaS0XnKjgei2q__vjF8VxqYN86JxAYCBKPKF33-fHwUmktKB_uPboRodPxGFYMqywNGVickcXXNeBepkdeNpQPMA5NBTB6YqsWPuiG_7xscu2qRrXiM1qdMJ729f59oWIDjcCCMhnSIjkeAPCZXecAN166TiSeGifTpBUUQfzCoTW1JAZt91n9tRkcCSMLon5HFFdfysW32ohULEzVT48W9kuSDxsftysK99RdmrQBDf0vhdU9Rrh0A5SO15PbILFHUOluu7T_7Z1k1YMHBEyVkb8Y3FgqJBzcSpwcsvrSsHf5wWyLCoyeND8RtBEQapzXks-crz2XmQuhWhJsSR-aDbs6Q3tJzQZRiP9H5hI7f0L7CPEvvvlNBxMCiwGIvltlEtNSllHDcUrjP2OlAUpCTYKE1HT0hE4ezMYAL3uTJfxqJgeWBKqbIz4eyp5rztZgE%3D) IX hearing aid so special?

It’s one of **the world’s first hearing aids with dual processing.**

This is special because it’s the first time engineers have been able to pack not one, but two state-of-the-art computer chips into a device this small.

The result? **An unprecedented amount of power and clarity.**

This means that [hear.com's](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c8c996b7a3c34d6babccd68cc6d90ff2&_e=cD_fCRaxny6ExYp8PO1FSL4RRUTh5orPkz8Y0QBEOaS0XnKjgei2q__vjF8VxqYN86JxAYCBKPKF33-fHwUmktKB_uPboRodPxGFYMqywNGVickcXXNeBepkdeNpQPMA5NBTB6YqsWPuiG_7xscu2qRrXiM1qdMJ729f59oWIDjcCCMhnSIjkeAPCZXecAN166TiSeGifTpBUUQfzCoTW1JAZt91n9tRkcCSMLon5HFFdfysW32ohULEzVT48W9kuSDxsftysK99RdmrQBDf0vhdU9Rrh0A5SO15PbILFHUOluu7T_7Z1k1YMHBEyVkb8Y3FgqJBzcSpwcsvrSsHfxjVD7WztyjlHz2C1OLEMxiaxOc-mXRLC4IR7Mg7ufuA-M4rlNz_ZRnVKLlycQ2L9e9ptGlJ70ZE7Gg_qvPaAehAU0jS-GLVm8p3TxR5QKyHYiP-nU3Xvr5WD0o_Lo-yAUkMCMPhiQk7GkDQqzbdbZY%3D) IX hearing aids offer all of the usual benefits of modern hearing aids—from smartphone connectivity to Bluetooth streaming—with the added bonus of something completely new: they can process speech and background noise independently, then recombine them for **unparalleled, crystal clear sound.**

“It's not just about volume,” explains [hear.com's](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=c8c996b7a3c34d6babccd68cc6d90ff2&_e=cD_fCRaxny6ExYp8PO1FSL4RRUTh5orPkz8Y0QBEOaS0XnKjgei2q__vjF8VxqYN86JxAYCBKPKF33-fHwUmktKB_uPboRodPxGFYMqywNGVickcXXNeBepkdeNpQPMA5NBTB6YqsWPuiG_7xscu2qRrXiM1qdMJ729f59oWIDjcCCMhnSIjkeAPCZXecAN166TiSeGifTpBUUQfzCoTW1JAZt91n9tRkcCSMLon5HFFdfysW32ohULEzVT48W9kuSDxsftysK99RdmrQBDf0vhdU9Rrh0A5SO15PbILFHUOluu7T_7Z1k1YMHBEyVkb8Y3FgqJBzcSpwcsvrSsHfw-6P9p1FCI6W-ceQ5LM4xUavW6c3TNazFnPNGh3DDYa7VyKiQMa1ug8-Gt8yimI5v5C0MCU0wUy5VsNsFmOHS366bXlBdPdWMoezIanCoxDd6rCJDFQRoO2qsX7EI7Bd58ZogvdSzV2X74gN8EYQJg%3D) founder Paul Crusius. “It's about speech clarity. And that's precisely what IX is designed to optimize.”

In other words: **with double the power comes double the clarity—and all that in a virtually invisible design!**

The problem: Most wait too long to act, hoping their hearing will improve on its own. Sadly, it never does. But now, a game-changing device is revolutionizing the hearing aid industry, and experts say it's the biggest breakthrough they've seen in over a decade. It's new IX hearing aid. And it’s the perfect solution at the perfect time—when more and more people are eager to maintain their quality of life and overcome these limitations. It’s no wonder IX hearing aid has taken the U.S. by storm since its release earlier this year offering hope and unprecedented clarity to thousands across America. What makes IX hearing aid so special? It’s one of the world’s first hearing aids with dual processing. This is special because it’s the first time engineers have been able to pack not one, but two state-of-the-art computer chips into a device this small. The result? An unprecedented amount of power and clarity. This means that IX hearing aids offer all of the usual benefits of modern hearing aids—from smartphone connectivity to Bluetooth streaming—with the added bonus of something completely new: they can process speech and background noise independently, then recombine them for unparalleled, crystal clear sound. “It's not just about volume,” explains founder Paul Crusius. “It's about speech clarity. And that's precisely what IX is designed to optimize.” In other words: with double the power comes double the clarity—and all that in a virtually invisible design! name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 1/8/2025
body:
Republicans in minority, but with House seat gains, as 75th General Assembly opens on Wednesday
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi folks,

Today is the first day of the 75th Colorado General Assembly, a pivotal moment for our state as lawmakers return to tackle the issues that matter most to Coloradans. From housing and education to energy and economic reform, this session promises to shape the future of our communities.

As always, Rocky Mountain Voice will keep you informed every step of the way, bringing you the latest updates, debates, and decisions from under the Gold Dome. Here’s to a productive and impactful session ahead!

And a summary of today's newsletter:

🏔️**Here in Colorado...** The [75th Colorado General Assembly](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) begins this week with Republicans gaining a few seats but remaining the minority party. Meanwhile, homelessness among Colorado families has [surged 134% over the past year](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). District 11 in Colorado Springs has [ended its decades-old contract](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) with the teachers' union, aiming to prioritize students and families in its education decisions. Additionally, after days of severe wintry weather, Denver International Airport reported over [250 canceled flights](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and thousands of delays.

For your reading beyond the newsletter, the ongoing placement of migrants has sparked a [heated clash](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) between the mayors of Denver and Aurora.

🇺🇸**On the National Stage...** President-elect Trump announced plans to [rename the Gulf of Mexico](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) to the "Gulf of America," sparking both intrigue and debate. Meta's Mark Zuckerberg revealed a major shift in Facebook's moderation policies, [scrapping third-party fact-checkers](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and moving toward a community-driven approach to content oversight.

🗣️ ******Commentary Spotlight...** Lindy Browning examines the impact of [Colorado’s new cage-free egg law](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) combined with avian flu outbreaks, which have left consumers facing skyrocketing prices and empty shelves.

This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, January 8, 2025.

– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Republicans in minority, but with House seat gains, as 75th General Assembly opens on Wednesday](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY BRIAN PORTER, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
The 75th session of the Colorado General Assembly opens Wednesday, Jan. 8, with sine die — adjournment on the final day of the session — slated for May 7.
Between, Republicans will be faced with minorities in both chambers and the Democratic trifecta, with Gov. Jared Polis heading up the executive branch.
Voters, though, did hand Republicans one advantage. House Republicans are no longer in a super minority and, like Senate Republicans, face a simple minority. That is not to say it will be any easier to pass Republican bills or defeat Democratic bills. Republicans face a 23-12 minority in the Senate and a 43-22 minority in the House.
Heading up the Republican House will again be Minority Leader Rose Pugliese of El Paso County. She has gained three seats from the prior session, when the Republican members were nicknamed the Mighty 19. Ty Winter of Las Animas County is the assistant minority leader. Ryan Armagost of Weld County will be in his first session as minority whip, a position previously held by Richard Holtorf of Washington County.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Homelessness among families with children in Colorado grew by 134% in 2024, according to federal report](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* A federal report revealed a 134% increase in family homelessness in Colorado during 2024, making it one of four states where family homelessness more than doubled.
* Rising inflation, stagnant wages, and the nationwide affordable housing crisis have exacerbated homelessness, with Colorado also seeing an influx of migrants, further straining resources.
* Advocates are urging increased investment in rapid rehousing and child care assistance programs to prevent further family displacement, emphasizing the urgent need for statewide solutions.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[In four days, wintry weather has canceled 250 flights, delayed 2,820 more at DIA](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* A four-day snowstorm in Denver caused major disruptions at Denver International Airport, with 250 flight cancellations and 2,820 delays, severely impacting air travel.
* SkyWest, Southwest, and United Airlines were hit hardest, accounting for the majority of cancellations and delays, particularly on Saturday and Sunday.
* As wintry conditions continue, passengers are urged to stay updated on flight statuses and prepare for potential delays or rebookings.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[District 11 ends decades-old contract with teacher union in effort to deliver for students, families](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* The Colorado Springs District 11 School Board voted 6-1 to end its decades-old master agreement with the Colorado Springs Education Association, citing the need for greater flexibility to address persistent challenges and improve student outcomes.
* The decision has divided the community, with supporters advocating for governance innovation and detractors raising concerns about the loss of protections for teachers and potential instability in classrooms.
* The district plans to replace the union agreement with a collaboratively developed employee handbook, aiming to maintain teacher protections while streamlining operations and fostering accountability.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Browning: Colorado egg law and bird flu has people ‘scrambling’ to find eggs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_During the last few weeks, there have been a couple of notable changes you may have noticed at your local grocer._
_The old saying that warns us not to keep all of our “eggs in one basket”, has now become, “I can’t find or afford eggs in my basket.” People are divided as to what has caused this “poaching” of their wallets, if they can find eggs on the shelf. No matter the cause, it has many people “fried.”_ ___Some people believe that the new Colorado law, which mandates that only cage-free eggs will be sold in Colorado, is to blame for the nearly-empty egg shelves and high cost of eggs, if you can find them. The “egg-ception” to the law is that egg-producing operations with 3.000 or fewer hens are not required to be cage-free._
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|

You don’t have to be a witness at a trial. In fact, once you make the accusation, you are essentially out of the process._ __
**[Letter: Reflections on America and the once thriving rural family farm economy](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****LETTER TO THE EDITOR****| COMMENTARY | JANUARY 6** _I have a small farm of 24 acres. Although only a fraction is cultivated, the rest is open grazing, and it keeps me plenty busy. I grow squash, rabbits, plum, hackberry, rose hips, mint, sage, potatoes, firewood, furniture wood, and hay, using horse and rabbit manure (and wood ash) for fertilizer._ __
